title: NHP Application Withdrawal Request Form - Canada.ca
content:
## NHP Application Withdrawal Request Form
NHP Product Licensing Bulletin No.5, October 27, 2025
- The Natural and Non-prescription Health Products Directorate (NNHPD) has developed a Withdrawal Request Form to ensure that requests for product licence application (PLA) withdrawals are processed in a consistent, efficient, and timely manner.
- The NHP Application Withdrawal Request Form replaces the current process for “Withdrawal of an Application”, outlined in the Natural Health Products Management of Applications Policy (NHP MAP) (2019 version) . An updated version of the NHP MAP will be published in Fall 2025 to reflect the updated process.
The NHP Application Withdrawal Request Form provides a streamlined process for applicants to request the withdrawal of a natural health product (NHP) licence application.
This bulletin only applies to NHP product licence applications (PLAs), including amendments.

## Withdrawal guidance:
The NHP Application Withdrawal Request Form is located at the following link: NHP Application Withdrawal Request Form
Effective immediately, to submit a request to withdraw a PLA, the NHP Application Withdrawal Request Form is now required.
The request must be made by the Senior Official of the company to withdraw a PLA. Alternatively, a designated party, for which a Designated Party Authorization Form (DPA) has been submitted to NNHPD, or a contact on the PLA, can withdraw a PLA on behalf of the Senior Official. Withdrawal requests from undesignated company representatives will not be processed.
This form is to be used to withdraw PLAs at any stage of the submission process, including applications pending review and withdrawals in response to an Information Request Notice (IRN).
Applicants are required to provide one of the following identifiers when completing the form:
- Submission number – found on the Acknowledgement Notice and/or the IRN issued for the application.
- PLA form tracking number For applications that have not yet been acknowledged (i.e., no submission number received), applicants can use the tracking number that appears at the top of the PLA webform once finalized.
- For applications that have not yet been acknowledged (i.e., no submission number received), applicants can use the tracking number that appears at the top of the PLA webform once finalized.
The NHP Application Withdrawal Request Form allows the applicant to identify one or multiple PLAs to be withdrawn. However, separate forms are required depending on the status of the applications being withdrawn:
- One form must be used for the withdrawal of applications that have a tracking number;
- A separate form must be used for the withdrawal of those with a submission number.
After completing and submitting the form, applicants will see a confirmation message that the form has been submitted. Once NNHPD processes the withdrawal, the applicant will receive an automated email from nnhpd.admin-admin.dpsnso@hc-sc.gc.ca confirming the withdrawal is complete. Please add the above noted email address to your trusted contact list to ensure you receive the automated email.
Applicants who requested the withdrawal of a PLA prior to the publication of this bulletin do not need to complete the NHP Application Withdrawal Request Form.
For inquiries about this bulletin, please contact us by email: nnhpd-dpsnso@hc-sc.gc.ca .
